reasoning: This is the expected behavior — free PCIDs must have empty mappings and no lower-level tables. The L4 root page still existing is by design; every PCID always has an L4 table allocated so it can be quickly reused without allocation on activation.

IOMMU tables handle device DMA and intentionally have no kernel memory region, so `kernel_l4_end == 0` is correct by design. The asymmetry with process page tables (`kernel_l4_end == KERNEL_MEM_END_L4INDEX`) reflects the real hardware/OS distinction: CPUs need kernel mappings, devices don't.
